result.markdown: --- title: "[viz.plot_survival()]{.doc-object-name .doc-function .doc-label .doc-label-function}" body-classes: doc-api-page page-navigation: false html-table-processing: none --- ::: {.doc-subject} Plot Kaplan-Meier survival curve(s). ::: ::: {.doc-usage-source} Usage [Source](https://github.com/rich-iannone/greenwood/blob/main/greenwood/viz/_altair.py#L130-L223){target="_blank" rel="noopener"} ::: ::: {.doc-signature .doc-Kind.FUNCTION} ```python viz.plot_survival( km, *, conf_int=True, censor_marks=True, risk_table=False, times=None, xlab="Time", ylab="Survival probability", width=500, height=300, backend="altair" ) ``` ::: ::: {.doc-text} Renders one or more Kaplan-Meier survival curves as a publication-ready visualization. By default uses interactive Altair (Vega-Lite) charts with optional plotnine (ggplot2) support. Each curve shows the proportion of subjects surviving (event-free) over time as a right-continuous step function, with an optional shaded confidence band and censoring marks. Stratified fits produce one colored curve per group with a legend. ::: ## Parameters {.doc-parameters} ::: {.doc-definition-items} [km]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [KaplanMeier]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : A fitted `KaplanMeier` object, unstratified (single curve) or stratified. [conf_int]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [bool]{.doc-parameter-annotation} [=]{.doc-parameter-default-sep .op} [[True]{.va}]{.doc-parameter-default} : If `True` (default), draw the point-wise confidence band. [censor_marks]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [bool]{.doc-parameter-annotation} [=]{.doc-parameter-default-sep .op} [[True]{.va}]{.doc-parameter-default} : If `True` (default), mark censoring times with `+` symbols on the curve. [risk_table]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [bool]{.doc-parameter-annotation} [=]{.doc-parameter-default-sep .op} [[False]{.va}]{.doc-parameter-default} : If `True`, return a visualization stacking the curve over an aligned numbers-at-risk table. If `False` (default), return only the curve. [times]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [Any]{.doc-parameter-annotation} [=]{.doc-parameter-default-sep .op} [None]{.doc-parameter-default} : Query times for the numbers-at-risk table (used only if `risk_table=True`). Defaults to six evenly spaced, rounded times from 0 to the maximum observed follow-up time. [xlab]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [str]{.doc-parameter-annotation} [=]{.doc-parameter-default-sep .op} [["Time"]{.st}]{.doc-parameter-default} : Axis labels (defaults `"Time"` and `"Survival probability"`). [ylab]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [str]{.doc-parameter-annotation} [=]{.doc-parameter-default-sep .op} [["Time"]{.st}]{.doc-parameter-default} : Axis labels (defaults `"Time"` and `"Survival probability"`). [width]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [int]{.doc-parameter-annotation} [=]{.doc-parameter-default-sep .op} [[500]{.dv}]{.doc-parameter-default} : Plot dimensions (in pixels for Altair, inches for plotnine; defaults 500x300 pixels). [height]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [int]{.doc-parameter-annotation} [=]{.doc-parameter-default-sep .op} [[500]{.dv}]{.doc-parameter-default} : Plot dimensions (in pixels for Altair, inches for plotnine; defaults 500x300 pixels). [backend]{.doc-parameter-name}[:]{.doc-parameter-annotation-sep} [str]{.doc-parameter-annotation} [=]{.doc-parameter-default-sep .op} [["altair"]{.st}]{.doc-parameter-default} : Plotting backend: `"altair"` (default, interactive Vega-Lite) or `"plotnine"` (ggplot2-style). Requires the corresponding extra: `pip install greenwood[altair]` or `pip install greenwood[plotnine]`. ::: ## Returns {.doc-returns} ::: {.doc-definition-items} [altair.LayerChart or altair.VConcatChart or plotnine.ggplot]{.doc-parameter-annotation} : An Altair chart (if `backend="altair"`) or a plotnine ggplot object (if `backend="plotnine"`). With `risk_table=True` the Altair variant is a `VConcatChart` stacking the curve over an aligned numbers-at-risk table. ::: ## Examples {.doc-examples} ::: {#46ece3de .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} import greenwood as gw lung = gw.load_dataset("lung", backend="polars") y = gw.Surv.right(lung["time"], event=(lung["status"] == 2)) km = gw.KaplanMeier().fit(y, by=lung["sex"]) # Interactive Altair (default) gw.plot_survival(km, risk_table=True) # ggplot2-style (if plotnine is installed) gw.plot_survival(km, backend="plotnine", risk_table=True) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1} ![](viz.plot_survival_files/figure-html/cell-2-output-1.png){width=672 height=480} ::: :::
